articleSummary: The article is a brief, informal tutorial aimed at developers who want to run Docker on macOS. Its core thesis is that Docker has become fast and reliable on OSX—especially after the 2016 switch from the legacy Boot2Docker VM to the native Docker‑for‑Mac (now Docker Desktop) client—so users should abandon the old, slow tooling and adopt the current native solution. It walks readers through a quick health‑check (docker info, port scanning, DNS caching, and choosing the proper storage driver such as overlay2) and recommends installing a recent Linux distro in a VM only as a fallback. The tone is conversational, peppered with humor (“c’mon those games aren’t helping your code”), and it concludes with a practical note on licensing (Docker Desktop is free for personal use) and an open‑source alternative (Rancher Desktop).
